title: DPIA Screening Thresholds
description: Criteria to determine when a Data Protection Impact Assessment is required under PDPA 2010

mandatoryTriggers.criterion: Large-scale processing of sensitive personal data
mandatoryTriggers.description: Processing sensitive data (s4: physical/mental health, political opinions, religious beliefs, criminal offences) affecting more than 10,000 data subjects
CCTV, facial recognition, or location tracking covering public spaces or employee workplaces at scale
Processing that produces legal or similarly significant decisions about individuals without human intervention (credit scoring, employment screening, insurance underwriting)
Transfer of personal data outside Malaysia to countries not specified by the Minister under s129(3)
Systematic processing of personal data of children under 18, particularly in educational technology, gaming, or social media contexts
Use of fingerprint, facial, iris, or voice biometrics for identification or authentication at scale

mandatoryTriggers.criterion: Data matching or combining from multiple sources
mandatoryTriggers.description: Combining personal data from multiple databases, systems, or organizations to create profiles or derive new insights

mandatoryTriggers.criterion: Processing that could result in discrimination
mandatoryTriggers.description: Use of personal data for profiling that could lead to exclusion, differential treatment, or social sorting based on protected characteristics
First-time use of new technologies (AI/ML, IoT, blockchain, gene sequencing) for processing personal data where impact is not yet fully understood

mandatoryTriggers.criterion: Processing preventing data subjects from exercising rights
mandatoryTriggers.description: Processing that could limit data subjects' ability to exercise their PDPA rights (access under s30, correction under s34, withdrawal of consent)

screeningQuestions.question: Does the processing involve sensitive personal data as defined in Section 4?
Will the processing affect more than 10,000 data subjects?
Does the processing involve systematic monitoring of individuals?
Will automated decisions be made that significantly affect individuals?
Does the processing involve cross-border data transfers?
Is this a new type of processing or use of new technology?
Does the processing involve data about children or vulnerable individuals?
Could the processing result in physical, financial, or reputational harm?
Will personal data be combined from multiple sources?
Does the processing involve biometric or genetic data?

bands.recommendation: DPIA not required — document screening decision
DPIA required — proceed with full assessment
